description: This figure contains two subplots of thermodynamic calculation results for Fe-9Mn-5Al-0.3C low-density δ-TRIP steel obtained via Thermo-Calc software with the TCFE 12 database. Subplot (a) plots the molar amount of four phases (FCC_A1 austenite, BCC_A2 δ-ferrite, cementite, liquid) as a function of temperature from room temperature to 1600 °C. Key features include: cementite precipitates below 617 °C, austenite fraction reaches a maximum of ~0.914 mol at ~970 °C, δ-ferrite precipitates from liquid at ~1485 °C, and austenite forms via peritectic reaction at ~1390 °C. Subplot (b) shows the mass fraction of Mn, Al, and C in ferrite and austenite as functions of temperature, revealing that Mn and C contents in austenite increase significantly below 970 °C until cementite precipitation at 617 °C, while Al content is higher in ferrite than in austenite. This calculation supports selection of 1050 °C as the intercritical quenching temperature and 620/680/740/800 °C as annealing temperatures, as high Al addition expands the δ-ferrite region so full austenitization cannot be achieved, and a wide ferrite-austenite dual-phase region exists.
